body:
_🛠️ Refactor suggestion_

**Disambiguate tests by package to avoid false flakiness**

`jq` aggregates by `.Test` only. Identically named tests across different packages will be merged and misreported as flaky/failing. Include the package when keying results.



```diff
-          jq -s '
-            map(select(.Action=="pass" or .Action=="fail") | select(.Test)) as $events
-            | reduce $events[] as $e ({}; .[$e.Test] = (
-                .[$e.Test] // {pass:0,fail:0}
-                | if $e.Action=="pass" then (.pass+=1) else (.fail+=1) end
-              )
-            )
-          ' json/*.json > json/counts.json
+          jq -s '
+            map(select(.Action=="pass" or .Action=="fail") | select(.Test)) as $events
+            | reduce $events[] as $e ({}; .[($e.Package // "unknown") + "/" + $e.Test] = (
+                .[($e.Package // "unknown") + "/" + $e.Test] // {pass:0,fail:0}
+                | if $e.Action=="pass" then (.pass+=1) else (.fail+=1) end
+              )
+            )
+          ' json/*.json > json/counts.json
```

The downstream summaries already use the map keys as names, so they’ll automatically include `pkg/test`.


Also applies to: 118-119

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
In .github/workflows/flake-go.yaml around lines 109 to 116 (and also the similar
block at lines 118-119), jq currently keys results by .Test only which merges
identically named tests across packages; change the key to include package (for
example use a concatenation like "\(.Package)/\(.Test)" or another pkg+test
unique identifier) in the map/select and the reduce assignment so counts are
stored per-package-per-test; update both occurrences so downstream summaries
receive unique pkg/test keys.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:chinchilla -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->
diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,146 @@
+name: go-flake-check
+
+on:
+  pull_request:
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+
+concurrency:
+  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
+  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.ref != 'refs/heads/main' }}
+
+jobs:
+  changed-go-tests:
+    runs-on: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'coder' && 'depot-ubuntu-22.04-8' || 'ubuntu-latest' }}
+    outputs:
+      any_changed: ${{ steps.changed.outputs.any_changed }}
+      files: ${{ steps.changed.outputs.all_changed_files }}
+    steps:
+      - name: Harden Runner
+        uses: step-security/harden-runner@ec9f2d5744a09debf3a187a3f4f675c53b671911 # v2.13.0
+        with:
+          egress-policy: audit
+
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@08eba0b27e820071cde6df949e0beb9ba4906955 # v4.3.0
+        with:
+          fetch-depth: 0
+
+      - id: changed
+        name: Detect changed go test files
+        uses: tj-actions/changed-files@v44
+        with:
+          files: |
+            **/*_test.go
+
+  go-flake:
+    needs: changed-go-tests
+    if: needs.changed-go-tests.outputs.any_changed == 'true'
+    runs-on: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'coder' && 'depot-ubuntu-22.04-8' || 'ubuntu-latest' }}
+    timeout-minutes: 30
+    env:
+      ATTEMPTS: '10'
+      POSTGRES_VERSION: '17'
+    steps:
+      - name: Harden Runner
+        uses: step-security/harden-runner@ec9f2d5744a09debf3a187a3f4f675c53b671911 # v2.13.0
+        with:
+          egress-policy: audit
+
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@08eba0b27e820071cde6df949e0beb9ba4906955 # v4.3.0
+        with:
+          fetch-depth: 1
+
+      - name: Setup Go
+        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-go
+
+      - name: Ensure jq
+        run: |
+          if ! command -v jq >/dev/null; then
+            sudo apt-get update -y
+            sudo apt-get install -y jq
+          fi
+
+      - name: Start Postgres (reuse CI helper)
+        run: |
+          make test-postgres-docker
+
+      - name: Build package and test regex from changed files
+        id: gometa
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          printf '%s\n' "${{ needs.changed-go-tests.outputs.files }}" | tr ' ' '\n' | sed '/^$/d' > /tmp/go_changed_files.txt
+          sort -u /tmp/go_changed_files.txt -o /tmp/go_changed_files.txt
+
+          pkgs=$(awk '{print $0}' /tmp/go_changed_files.txt | xargs -I{} dirname {} | sort -u | while read d; do (cd "$d" && go list -f '{{.ImportPath}}'); done)
+          if [ -z "$pkgs" ]; then
+            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            exit 0
+          fi
+          echo "pkgs=$pkgs" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+
+          tests=$(awk '{print $0}' /tmp/go_changed_files.txt | xargs -I{} sh -c "sed -nE 's/^func[[:space:]]+(Test[[:alnum:]_]+)[[:space:]]*\\(.*/\\1/p' {}" | sort -u | paste -sd'|' -)
+          if [ -z "$tests" ]; then
+            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            exit 0
+          fi
+          echo "regex=^(${tests})$" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+
+      - name: Run repeated tests to detect flakiness
+        if: steps.gometa.outputs.skip != 'true'
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          mkdir -p flake/go json
+          regex='${{ steps.gometa.outputs.regex }}'
+          pkgs='${{ steps.gometa.outputs.pkgs }}'
+
+          for i in $(seq 1 ${ATTEMPTS}); do
+            for p in $pkgs; do
+              go test -json -run "$regex" -count=1 -shuffle=on "$p" \
+                | tee "json/run-$i-$(echo "$p" | tr '/.' '__').json" >/dev/null || true
+            done
+          done
+
+          jq -s '
+            map(select(.Action=="pass" or .Action=="fail") | select(.Test)) as $events
+            | reduce $events[] as $e ({}; .[$e.Test] = (
+                .[$e.Test] // {pass:0,fail:0}
+                | if $e.Action=="pass" then (.pass+=1) else (.fail+=1) end
+              )
+            )
+          ' json/*.json > json/counts.json

body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_

**Syntax bug: missing 'fi' and echo escape not interpreted**

The bash block lacks a closing `fi`, which will fail the step. Also `echo "\n..."` won’t render a newline without `-e`. Use `printf`.



```diff
-          echo "\nConsistently failing tests:" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
+          printf "\nConsistently failing tests:\n" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
           if jq -e 'length==0' json/failing.json >/dev/null; then
             echo "  none" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
           else
             jq -r '.[] | "  \(.name): fail=\(.fail)"' json/failing.json >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
+          fi
```

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
          printf "\nConsistently failing tests:\n" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
          if jq -e 'length==0' json/failing.json >/dev/null; then
            echo "  none" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
          else
            jq -r '.[] | "  \(.name): fail=\(.fail)"' json/failing.json >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
          fi
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
.github/workflows/flake-go.yaml around lines 128 to 133: the bash conditional is
missing its closing "fi" and the echo using "\n" won't produce a newline; close
the if block with a matching "fi" after the else branch, and replace the echo
that writes the leading newline with printf (or echo -e) so the newline escape
is interpreted (e.g. use printf '\nConsistently failing tests:\n' >>
"$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"), keeping the rest of the jq/echo redirections as-is.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:chinchilla -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Addressed in commits 462e612 to ca1bd6c

+          sort -u /tmp/go_changed_files.txt -o /tmp/go_changed_files.txt

+            map(select(.Action=="pass" or .Action=="fail") | select(.Test)) as $events
+            | reduce $events[] as $e ({}; .[$e.Test] = (
+                .[$e.Test] // {pass:0,fail:0}
+                | if $e.Action=="pass" then (.pass+=1) else (.fail+=1) end
+              )
+            )
+          ' json/*.json > json/counts.json
+
+          jq 'to_entries | map(select(.value.fail>0 and .value.pass>0) | {name:.key, pass:.value.pass, fail:.value.fail})' json/counts.json > json/flaky.json

+          fi

+          echo "\nConsistently failing tests:" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
+          if jq -e 'length==0' json/failing.json >/dev/null; then
+            echo "  none" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"

body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_

**Test case mismatch: input port (11) vs expected (2113) will always fail**

The "127.0.0.1" case now passes "127.0.0.1:11" but still expects 2113 with wantErr=false. Either revert the input to 127.0.0.1:2113 or change want to 11. Suggest reverting to keep intent clear.



Apply one of the following diffs (pick one):

Option A — revert input to match expected:
```diff
-            urlString: "127.0.0.1:11",
+            urlString: "127.0.0.1:2113",
             want:      2113,
```

Option B — update expected to match input:
```diff
-            urlString: "127.0.0.1:11",
-            want:      2113,
+            urlString: "127.0.0.1:11",
+            want:      11,
```

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
         {
             name:      "127.0.0.1",
            urlString: "127.0.0.1:2113",
             want:      2113,
             wantErr:   false,
         },
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
In cli/agent_internal_test.go around lines 50 to 54, the test case uses
urlString "127.0.0.1:11" but expects want 2113 which will always fail; fix by
making the input and expectation consistent: either revert urlString to
"127.0.0.1:2113" to keep want=2113 (Option A) or change want to 11 to match the
current urlString (Option B); pick one and update the test case so urlString and
want match and keep wantErr as false.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:chinchilla -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Addressed in commits 462e612 to ca1bd6c

_⚠️ Potential issue_

**Guard artifact upload when test run was skipped**

When `gometa.skip == 'true'`, no `json/` dir exists and this step will fail despite earlier intentional skip. Gate the upload on the same condition while preserving `always()` for failure cases.

```diff
-      - name: Upload artifacts
-        if: always()
+      - name: Upload artifacts
+        if: ${{ always() && steps.gometa.outputs.skip != 'true' }}
         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2
         with:
           name: go-flake-json
           path: json/
```

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
      - name: Upload artifacts
        if: ${{ always() && steps.gometa.outputs.skip != 'true' }}
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2
        with:
          name: go-flake-json
          path: json/
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
.github/workflows/flake-go.yaml around lines 135 to 141: the artifact upload
step unconditionally runs even when gometa.skip == 'true' and the json/
directory doesn't exist; change the step's if so it still runs for
cleanup/failure (always()) but only attempts upload when the job wasn't
intentionally skipped by combining always() with the existing skip check (e.g.,
if: always() && github.event.inputs.gometa.skip != 'true' or the same
expression/variable used earlier in the workflow), ensuring the upload is gated
by the same condition used for skipping tests.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:triton:chinchilla -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

body:

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

**Do not land randomized failures in core tests; gate or remove.**

Introducing a 10% random failure makes the suite nondeterministic and will create spurious red builds outside the flake-check workflow. Additionally, because math/rand uses a deterministic default seed, this may never actually trigger on CI, undermining the intended signal.

Recommended fixes (pick one):

- Option A — remove the injection entirely (safe default for main):
```diff
-		// Random failure injection - 10% chance
-		if rand.Float64() < 0.1 {
-			t.Fatal("Random test failure for testing purposes")
-		}
```

- Option B — gate injection behind an env var and use cryptographic randomness so the workflow can opt-in without affecting normal CI:
```diff
-		// Random failure injection - 10% chance
-		if rand.Float64() < 0.1 {
-			t.Fatal("Random test failure for testing purposes")
-		}
+		// Optional flake injection for flake-check workflow runs (off by default).
+		if pStr := os.Getenv("CODER_TEST_INJECT_FLAKE_PERCENT"); pStr != "" {
+			p, err := strconv.Atoi(pStr)
+			require.NoError(t, err)
+			f, err := cryptorand.Float64()
+			require.NoError(t, err)
+			if f < float64(p)/100.0 {
+				t.Fatalf("Random test failure injected (%d%%)", p)
+			}
+		}
```

Follow-up for Option B:
- Set CODER_TEST_INJECT_FLAKE_PERCENT (e.g., 10) solely in the go-flake-check job, not in regular test jobs.

```shell
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "1) Check math/rand usage in tests:"
rg -nP -C2 --type=go '\brand\.Float64\(|"math/rand"' -- '**/*_test.go' || echo "OK: no math/rand usage in tests."

echo
echo "2) Inspect flake workflow for env gating:"
rg -nP -C3 '\.github/workflows/flake-go\.ya?ml' -l || echo "flake-go workflow file not found."
if fd -H '.github/workflows/flake-go.y*ml' >/dev/null; then
  fd -H '.github/workflows/flake-go.y*ml' -X bash -lc 'echo "--- {} ---"; rg -nP -C3 "CODER_TEST_INJECT_FLAKE_PERCENT|go-flake|changed-go-tests|no-changed-tests" "{}"'
fi
```

**Remove uncontrolled random failure in cli/agent_test.go**  

The snippet in cli/agent_test.go (around line 39) injects a 10% chance of failure using math/rand.Float64, making the test suite nondeterministic and liable to produce spurious red builds without flake-check. Moreover, the CI workflows do not currently set any gating variable, so this code will silently never fire on CI (due to the deterministic default seed), defeating its purpose.

Please pick one of the following fixes:

• Option A – Remove the injection entirely (safe default for all workflows):  
```diff
--- a/cli/agent_test.go
+++ b/cli/agent_test.go
@@ -36,6 +36,3 @@ func TestSomething(t *testing.T) {
-       // Random failure injection – 10% chance
-       if rand.Float64() < 0.1 {
-           t.Fatal("Random test failure for testing purposes")
-       }
}
```

• Option B – Gate flake injection behind an env var and use crypto/rand so only flake-check jobs opt in:  
```diff
--- a/cli/agent_test.go
+++ b/cli/agent_test.go
@@ -36,6 +36,17 @@ func TestSomething(t *testing.T) {
-       // Random failure injection – 10% chance
-       if rand.Float64() < 0.1 {
-           t.Fatal("Random test failure for testing purposes")
-       }
+       // Optional flake injection (off by default)
+       if pStr := os.Getenv("CODER_TEST_INJECT_FLAKE_PERCENT"); pStr != "" {
+           p, err := strconv.Atoi(pStr)
+           require.NoError(t, err)
+           f, err := cryptorand.Float64()
+           require.NoError(t, err)
+           if f < float64(p)/100.0 {
+               t.Fatalf("Injected flake failure (%d%%)", p)
+           }
+       }
}
```

Follow-up for Option B:  
• In .github/workflows/flake-go.yaml, in the job that runs go-flake-check, set  
```yaml
env:
  CODER_TEST_INJECT_FLAKE_PERCENT: 10
```  
and do **not** include this env var in regular CI jobs.  

These changes are critical to preserving deterministic test behavior and ensuring any flake injection runs only when explicitly enabled.
